title: [META][Adversarial review] Home-user coordination system upgrade tracker — April 2026 review
body:
## Summary

This meta issue tracks the outcome of a top-to-bottom adversarial review performed on 2026-04-23 against `main` (and `claude/security-review-agent-system-sXMRZ`). The review was framed around one question: **"What would it take to make Maxwell-Daemon the ultimate agent coordination system for home users to interface with their AI models?"** Every finding below is backed by file:line references in the source tree.

This is not a substitute for the shipping epics (#274, #21) or the domain epics (#392-#399). It is a gap list of things those epics do *not* cover or that need a concrete ticket to move.

## Top-line findings

1. **Critical marketing/reality gap**: `README.md:11-46` claims Docker `--network none` isolation; `sandbox/runner.py:41-82` is a plain subprocess wrapper. See #468.
2. **Cross-platform onboarding is broken**: `Launch-Maxwell.bat` is Windows-only; there is no macOS / Linux launcher. See #469.
3. **The launched UI is non-functional**: `gui/app.py` is a static PyQt placeholder; three overlapping UI stacks (PyQt, Electron, web). See #470.
4. **No first-run wizard**: Users must hand-edit YAML to configure even a single backend. See #471.
5. **Secrets in plaintext YAML**: No OS keyring; `SecretStr` is not encryption. See #472.
6. **JWT lifecycle gaps**: No revocation, no refresh, no brute-force defence. See #473.
7. **Concurrency hazards**: Thread-unsafe queue use, event suppression, disk-write swallow, config-reload races. See #474, #489.
8. **CI is red** (trivial lint fail + `pip-audit` non-blocking). See #475.

## Issue index (created by this review)

### P0 — blocking for a real home-user release
- #468 — Sandbox: subprocess pretending to be Docker.
- #469 — Windows-only launcher; no macOS/Linux story.
- #470 — PyQt6 GUI placeholder; UI fragmentation.
- #471 — First-run onboarding wizard.
- #472 — Plaintext secrets → OS keyring.
- #473 — JWT lifecycle (revocation, refresh, brute-force).
- #474 — PriorityQueue concurrency races + related bugs.
- #475 — CI: lint failure + supply-chain gating + coverage floor.

### P1 — required to beat existing home-user tools
- #476 — Add Gemini, Groq, Mistral, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Together, HuggingFace backends.
- #477 — MCP (Model Context Protocol) client + server support.
- #478 — Local-model discovery (Ollama `/api/tags`, LM Studio, vLLM) + benchmarking.
- #479 — Streaming for CLI-wrapper backends.
- #480 — Complexity-, capability-, and latency-aware routing with empirical scores.
- #481 — Cost estimator + real-time budget alerts + per-workspace rollups.
- #482 — Persistent multi-turn conversation context.
- #483 — Autostart (systemd / launchd / Windows Service) + auto-update.
- #484 — Backup, restore, and export.
- #485 — Per-backend concurrency limits + circuit breaker + Retry-After.
- #486 — End-to-end tracing and correlation IDs.
- #488 — Task dependencies, DAGs, cron, non-GitHub webhooks.

### P2 — polish and breadth
- #487 — Bound in-memory task dict; ledger pool; stream history.
- #489 — Config hot-reload races with in-flight requests.
- #490 — Audit-log nested redaction + brute-force rate-limiter + ID regex tightening.
- #491 — Test coverage gaps (sandbox, webhook, JWT, rate limiter).
- #492 — Dry-run mode, rollback/undo, task templates.
- #493 — VS Code / JetBrains / Zed / Obsidian extensions.
- #494 — Model benchmark + leaderboard UI driven by existing evals.
- #496 — Mobile-responsive web UI + PWA.
- #497 — Supply-chain hardening (sigstore, SBOM, pinning, dependabot policy).
- #498 — Repo-local vs personal memory with privacy controls.
- #499 — Voice input, image attachments, multimodal task submission.

## Not-covered-here (already being worked)

- #274 — Next-Gen Autonomous Architecture epic.
- #21 — Beta release.
- #19 — Comprehensive docs site.
- #289 — External agent adapter/plugin framework.
- #392 — Gate runtime.
- #393 — Critic panel.
- #394 — Subscription-aware resource broker.
- #395 — Delegate lifecycle with leases.
- #396 — Fleet capability registry.
- #397 — Repo-carried memory + fleet sync.
- #398 — Gate-aware dashboard.
- #399 — Validation sandbox gauntlet policies.

Several of these (#392–#399) sit *on top of* the P0/P1 foundations above. If the foundations don't land first, the polished features are built on a confidence-destroying base.

## Recommended sequencing

Six weeks of focused work in this order (each bullet can go in parallel within the group):

**Weeks 1-2 (P0 blockers)**
- #468 Docker sandbox, #472 keyring, #473 JWT lifecycle.
- #475 CI fix.
- #474 concurrency fix.

**Weeks 3-4 (Home-user surface)**
- #469 cross-OS launcher, #470 single UI story, #471 onboarding wizard.
- #476 Gemini/Groq/LM Studio backends, #478 local discovery, #479 CLI streaming.
- #483 autostart, #484 backup/restore.

**Weeks 5-6 (Differentiators)**
- #477 MCP, #482 conversations, #481 cost estimator, #480 smart routing, #485 concurrency limits.
- #486 tracing, #488 triggers, #492 templates + dry-run.
- #494 benchmark leaderboard, #499 multimodal, #493 VS Code extension.

## Process note

Every issue carries:
- A `Product outcome` section (what the home user experiences after the fix).
- A `Problem` section with concrete file:line references to the current code.
- A `Proposed resolution` with specific API/UI/module-level changes.
- `Acceptance criteria` that CI or a reviewer can check against.
- `Related` links to existing open/closed issues to preserve context.

## Review artefacts

The review itself explored:
- Core orchestration architecture (`maxwell_daemon/daemon/`, `core/`, `director/`, `fleet/`, `api/`, `gui/`).
- Backends + routing + tools + sandbox + browser + SSH.
- Auth, audit, tracing, GitHub integration, webhooks.
- Tests, CI workflows, deployment assets.
- Existing open and closed issues (191 closed, 12 open before this review).

No existing open issue was duplicated. Where a fix sits on top of an existing epic, the new issue links it and narrows the scope to the specific user-visible gap.

performed_via_github_app.name: Claude
performed_via_github_app.description:
Run Claude Code from your GitHub Pull Requests and Issues to respond to reviewer feedback, fix CI errors, or modify code, turning it into a virtual teammate that works alongside your development pipelines.

This is built on the publicly available Claude Code SDK.
